Document:
# Precautionary Charges These are used to confine people indefinitely, similar to capital crimes.
## Insanity Acting in a manner that indicates the individual is not of sound mind and poses a danger to themselves or others (e.g., attempting suicide). This does not include individuals reporting harmless or non-disruptive experiences, such as having an “imaginary friend.” - Confinement may be applied for up to 15 minutes. During this time, the individual is to be evaluated by a medical professional. Following evaluation, the individual is to be released or, if deemed unfit, discharged from the unit.
## Incompetence for Duty A status applied to an arrested military member charged solely with the following capital crimes: Attempted Murder, Desertion, Espionage, or Jailbreak. This status may not be applied to any other capital offences. - The individual will be stripped of rank and military equipment and discharged from the unit.
## Prisoner of War Any detained combatant as defined in the [textlink="Definitions and Glossary" link="AU14UCMJDefinitionsandGlossary"]. - Discretionary confinement, usually permanent
